channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A record number of political parties register for Haiti's first election in a decade
item.description: A record 280 political parties had registered by Thursday's deadline to participate in Haiti's first general election in a decade, hopeful for a chance to help ease their country's multiple crises.
item.content:encoded:
A record 280 political parties had registered by Thursday's deadline to participate in Haiti's first general election in a decade, hopeful for a chance to help ease their country's multiple crises.

(Image credit: Odelyn Joseph)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: NYC's Mamdani condemns Tuberville's anti-Muslim posts as "bigotry"
item.description: Speaking at a public iftar dinner, held to break the daily Ramadan fast, New York City Mayor Mamdani described Sen. Tuberville's anti-Muslim rhetoric as "bigotry" and "hatred."
item.content:encoded:
Speaking at a public iftar dinner, held to break the daily Ramadan fast, New York City Mayor Mamdani described Sen. Tuberville's anti-Muslim rhetoric as "bigotry" and "hatred."

(Image credit: Andres Kudacki)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: China slams Trump's trade investigation, as it approves a 5-year economic plan
item.description: China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.
item.content:encoded:
China's Foreign Ministry criticized the Trump administration's trade investigation as a "pretext" for tariffs. Meanwhile, China is moving ahead with a five-year plan that may rankle trade partners.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: NASA targets Artemis II crewed moon mission for April 1 launch
item.description: A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.
item.content:encoded:
A six-day launch window opens on April 1 from NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The lunar orbital mission would be the first time humans have returned to the moon since Apollo 17 in 1972.

(Image credit: Gregg Newton)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Fear of Iranian mines in the Strait of Hormuz could further slow the flow of oil
item.description: Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.
item.content:encoded:
Attacks by Iran have already nearly halted the flow of oil through the vital waterway as commercial ship crews fear being hit by missiles, drones or mines.

(Image credit: Handout/Royal Thai Navy)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: FBI says its taking over the Michigan synagogue attack investigation
item.description: Security officers at Temple Israel "engaged with the suspect" after a vehicle rammed into the building, according to Oakland County Sheriff Mike Bouchard.
item.content:encoded:
Security officers at Temple Israel "engaged with the suspect" after a vehicle rammed into the building, according to Oakland County Sheriff Mike Bouchard.

(Image credit: Emily Elconin)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: This tale of a Chicago school book ban was inspired by true events
item.description: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.content:encoded: Librarian Jarrett Dapier's graphic novel tells a fictionalized account of real-life events in 2013 that restricted access to Marjane Satrapi's memoir Persepolis in Chicago Public Schools.
item.dc:creator: Tahneer Oksman

item.title: Medicare Advantage 'dark money' group tries to win higher payments for insurers
item.description: What appeared to be a surge of grassroots support for higher Medicare Advantage payments was actually driven by a pro-industry group.
item.content:encoded:
What appeared to be a surge of grassroots support for higher Medicare Advantage payments was actually driven by a pro-industry group.

(Image credit: Eric Harkleroad/KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Fred Schulte

item.title: Senate passes bipartisan housing bill targeting large investors and easing regulations
item.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act would ban large investors from buying up single-family homes.
item.content:encoded:
The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act would ban large investors from buying up single-family homes.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: This reporter went bust while covering America's sports betting boom
item.description: Americans are betting on sports, elections, award shows and even military actions. The Atlantic writer McKay Coppins bet $10k from his employer in his investigation of this gambling world.
item.content:encoded: Americans are betting on sports, elections, award shows and even military actions. The Atlantic writer McKay Coppins bet $10k from his employer in his investigation of this gambling world.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley
